name: Upside Down Apple Pie
recipeIngredient: 5 cups Apples, peeled, cored and sliced
recipeInstructions:
1. Line a 9-inch pie plate with a wide piece of foil.
2. Cut two pieces of 12-inch foil, about 2 feet long. Fold each lengthwise to form one large square. Press into the pie plate, ensuring it covers the bottom and sides up to the top edge.
3. Place pecan or walnut halves on the side of the foil in the center of the pie plate.
4. Sprinkle brown sugar over the nuts.
5. In a large bowl, combine granulated sugar, flour, cinnamon, salt, and lemon juice. Stir well until smooth.
6. Add the apples to the dry mixture and stir until combined. Spread evenly over the nuts.
7. Roll out the pastry dough and place it over the filling. Fold edges under and flute. This creates a rim to hold juice when the pie is turned upside down.
8. Turn up the foil around the pastry, leaving about 4 inches (inches) at the top.
9. Bake in a 350°F oven for about 55 minutes or until the apples are tender.
10. Remove the foil carefully and let cool before serving.
